methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Ocean Infinity, a Growth-stage maritime and ocean technology company founded by Oliver Plunkett, has raised over $100M to date. The comparable universe is limited: no public peers are available, and only one identifiable private comparable—Orbital Marine Power—exists within the ocean and maritime sector.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here, using total capital raised as a valuation proxy. Starting from the $100M raised baseline, a typical 1.2x step-up multiplier applied to companies advancing from Growth stage yields an implied valuation of $120M. This methodology assumes the company has deployed capital efficiently and achieved milestones consistent with Growth-stage progression. The scarcity of comparable transactions and public benchmarks constrains the ability to cross-validate via revenue or EBITDA multiples.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $102M (low) to $138M (high), with a median of $120M. The narrow band reflects the limited comparative data rather than high confidence. Key uncertainties include: lack of public comps to establish sector-wide multiples, reliance on a single private comparable, the absence of disclosed revenue or unit economics for Ocean Infinity itself, and the nascent state of commercial deployment in ocean technology sectors. Any valuation beyond $120M warrants evidence of material revenue growth, customer traction, or technology de-risking not reflected in the available data.
